data: Iscrizioni anelleniche di Sicilia. II. Sicilia Centrale e Meridionale

Orsi, 1876-, Notizie degli scavi di antichità
Text encoding: Valentina Mignosa, edition source:
The inscription is placed on the upper part of the body of the askos. The text is arranged in two lines, the first runs clockwise, the second counter-clockwise.
first half of the 5th century BCE (De Ligt 2007; Morandi 1982; Pulgram 1978; Schmoll 1958; Whatmough 1933); second half of the 6th century BCE (Agostiniani 1992)
de Ligt, 2007, The Inscription from Centuripe: Language, Meaning and Historical Background
Agostiniani, 1992, Les parlers indigenes de la Sicile pregrecque
Pulgram, 1978, Italic, Latin, Italian, 600 BC to AD 1260. Texts and commentaries
Whatmough, 1933, The Prae-Italic Dialects of Italy
The inscription runs slightly below the protruding edge of the tile, on the front of it (the exposed part).
end of the 6th—beginning of the 5th century BCE (Biondi 2002).
Biondi, 2002, Iscrizione arcaica su laterizio dalla chora di Centuripe
end of the 6th—beginning of the 5th century BCE
The inscription runs along the back of the bust, at the height of the right shoulder of the female figure.
Manenti, 2010, Una dedica su un busto da Grammichele?
private (graffito), label indicating the content of the vessel (?)
The text is scratched on the outside of the body of the vessel.

The text is scratched on the underside of the mug.

The text is scratched on the outer surface of the vessel.
The text is scratched on the wall of the vessel.

The text is painted on the wall of the vessel, arranged in two parallel bands spaced out by a geometric decoration.
                                            The two strips have about the same number of letters and are arranged symmetrically to one another.

The text is arranged in a clockwise spiral and there is a letter chi (or a commercial mark) at the center of the inscription that does not seem part of the text.

                    as this text appeared to be a different inscription due to the difference in the reading of the ductus of alpha (see Arena IGASM 2, 92, fig. 27.3; Agostiniani 2018, p. 183-184, fig. 89) but it is actually the same document (see commentary in ISic020558).
